# Visual notes — Layers of AI

## Layout

Vertical stack of six translucent cylinders on a dark tech background (network lines, binary). Title centered at top: **Layers of AI**. Bottom-right credit: profile photo + "Follow Michael - From Pilots to Platforms".

## Layers (bottom → top)

| # | Layer | Color (approx) | Labels / icons visible |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Classical AI | dark blue | Symbolic AI; Expert Systems; Knowledge Representation; Logic & Reasoning |
| 2 | Machine Learning | cyan | Supervised; Unsupervised; Classification; Reinforcement Learning; Regression |
| 3 | Neural Networks | green | Perceptrons; Cost Functions; Backpropagation; Activation Functions; Hidden Layers |
| 4 | Deep Learning | orange | Transformers; LSTMs; RNNs; CNNs; Autoencoders |
| 5 | Generative AI | magenta | LLMs; Diffusion Models; Multimodal (audio/image icons); VAEs |
| 6 | Agentic AI | purple | Memory; Planning; Tool Use; Autonomous Execution |

## Implied claim (from stacking)

Each ring sits on the one below → suggests historical/capability buildup ending in agentic systems.

## Ambiguities / caution

- Stack ≠ dependency: production agents still use classical rules, ML classifiers, and non-generative tools; GenAI is not a required substrate for every agent.
- "Autonomous Execution" on the top ring is marketing-adjacent; XingAI courses stress *bounded* authority and approval gates (Course 03/04), not unbounded autonomy.
- Transformers appear under Deep Learning while LLMs appear under Generative AI — overlapping, not clean partitions.
- No sources/citations on the graphic; treat as pedagogy aid, not taxonomy authority.

## Agent interpretation

Primary wiki value is mapping this popular stack onto the XingAI curriculum (which mostly lives in Generative + Agentic bands, with Course 00 covering the lower vocabulary) and naming where the graphic oversimplifies enterprise work.
